title: Bridge Essay: The functions of the temporal-parietal junction.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through The, functions, of, the, temporal-parietal.

**The functions of the temporal-parietal junction.** — Handb Clin Neurol. PMID: 35964969. DOI: 10.1016/B978-0-12-823493-8.00020-1. In the human brain, the temporal-parietal junction (TPJ) is ahistologically heterogenous area that includes the ventral portions of the parietal cortex and the caudal superior temporal gyrus sector adjacent to the posterior end of the Sylvian fissure. The anatomical heterogeneity of the TPJ is matched by its seemingly ubiquitous involvement in different cognitive functions that span from memory to language, attention, self-consciousness, and social behavior.
